title: Probability Models and Stochastic Processes · Quantity Clinic: Markov Chains
summary: Build, test, and communicate a model involving reliability and arrival processes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math adv markov chains increment in a reproducible stochastic models computational study. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate markov chains in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute limit theorems in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Markov Chains evidence
steps.instruction: An outcome pays $35 with probability 0.35 and $0 otherwise. Calculate its expected value.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Weight each outcome by its probability: 35 × 0.35 + 0 × 0.65.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Weight each outcome by its probability: 35 × 0.35 + 0 × 0.65.
